note: Implemented entries are runnable from this repository. Planned entries are public benchmarks that require optional datasets, services, or heavier dependencies.

benchmarks.name: Agent user-memory retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: 200 synthetic user facts, 50 natural-language Russian queries
benchmarks.target: Match Chroma precision@1 on static recall, beat it on precision@3, and keep avg latency below 5 ms at 200 memories.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same benchmark with sentence-transformers and a FAISS-backed candidate index.

benchmarks.name: Agent coherence and token savings
benchmarks.dataset: 500-memory long user history with corrections, TTL, namespace isolation, project recall, and repeated personalization tasks
benchmarks.target: Show that dynamic memory improves agent behavior, not only retrieval: higher task success, fewer stale errors, longer coherent runs, compact context, and observable Memory OS learning signals.
benchmarks.next_step: Move this scenario from deterministic task scoring to LLM answer-quality scoring on LoCoMo/LongMemEval-style tasks.

benchmarks.name: Dynamic memory policy
benchmarks.dataset: Hot memory, TTL, correction, and namespace checks over 200 memories
benchmarks.target: Keep precision@1 and stale suppression at 1.00 while reducing avg latency below 10 ms at 1000 memories.
benchmarks.next_step: Add Chroma metadata-policy and Qdrant payload-filter baselines so the comparison is not only static-vector search.

benchmarks.name: Field memory graph dynamics
benchmarks.dataset: 13 deterministic memories: conflicting facts, related concept memories, activation spreading, inhibition, and decay
benchmarks.target: Keep graph precision@1, stale suppression, concept formation, and concept consolidation at 1.00 while moving the same memory dynamics into LoCoMo/LongMemEval evidence tasks.
benchmarks.next_step: Make MemoryFieldGraph incremental and evaluate conflict/update behavior on public long-memory datasets.

benchmarks.name: WaveMind capacity curve
benchmarks.dataset: Static and dynamic agent-memory checks at 200, 1000, and 5000 memories
benchmarks.target: Hold precision@1 >= 0.95 at 5000 memories and avg dynamic query latency below 20 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Move candidate generation to FAISS/Annoy and limit wave-field reranking to the top candidate set.

benchmarks.name: Long-term memory evidence
benchmarks.dataset: Synthetic long-memory evidence scenario with profile, preference, correction, TTL, namespace, and filler history
benchmarks.target: Keep this as a small regression check for stale suppression, correction, namespace isolation, and personalization.
benchmarks.next_step: Use public LoCoMo and LongMemEval for external claims; keep this synthetic scenario for fast regression testing.

benchmarks.name: BEIR-style open retrieval runner
benchmarks.dataset: Any local BEIR-style corpus.jsonl, queries.jsonl, and qrels/<split>.tsv dataset.
benchmarks.target: Run WaveMind, Chroma, and Qdrant with identical embeddings and compare retrieval/index behavior on public qrels.
benchmarks.next_step: Add sentence-transformers runs for SciFact, then add NFCorpus as the second BEIR dataset.

benchmarks.name: NoMIRACL Russian retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: NoMIRACL Russian test relevant subset, 200 queries, 5000 compact top-k candidate passages, human-annotated relevance labels.
benchmarks.target: Reach same-embedding Russian nDCG@10 parity with Chroma/Qdrant while moving latency below 5 ms through FAISS/service-mode candidate indexes.
benchmarks.next_step: Run sentence-transformers on the same NoMIRACL Russian split and then add the full MIRACL Russian corpus profile when disk/service capacity allows it.

benchmarks.name: LoCoMo evidence retrieval runner
benchmarks.dataset: Official LoCoMo locomo10.json, using conversation turns as memories and QA evidence dialog ids as relevance labels.
benchmarks.target: Improve LoCoMo evidence_recall@5 with semantic embeddings and keep retrieval latency below 20 ms for WaveMind.
benchmarks.next_step: Add LoCoMo answer generation with a local LLM and measure answer accuracy/faithfulness.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval evidence retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: Official LongMemEval-S cleaned file, 470 non-abstention questions, 22419 session memories, session-level evidence retrieval.
benchmarks.target: Keep full LongMemEval-S evidence recall above static vector-store baselines while staying below 20 ms retrieval latency.
benchmarks.next_step: Run turn-level evidence mode and sentence-transformers, then add LLM answer accuracy/abstention evaluation.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval evidence 50-query smoke
benchmarks.dataset: Official LongMemEval-S cleaned file, first 50 non-abstention questions, session-level evidence retrieval.
benchmarks.target: Keep this smoke profile above Chroma/Qdrant evidence recall while using it as the fast regression check before full LongMemEval reruns.
benchmarks.next_step: Speed up full LongMemEval reruns by reusing per-question candidate indexes or adding a streaming runner mode.

benchmarks.name: ANN index latency curve
benchmarks.dataset: Generated normalized 128-d vectors at 1000, 5000, 10000, and 50000 points; recall@10 measured against exact cosine neighbors.
benchmarks.target: At 50000 vectors, keep recall@10 above 0.95 while reducing latency below exact NumPy or move this role to a production vector index.
benchmarks.next_step: Tune quantized search kernels, add FAISS on Linux/macOS CI, and test Qdrant service-mode curves beyond 50000 vectors.

benchmarks.name: Production index profile
benchmarks.dataset: Docker-backed 50000-vector profile comparing persisted FAISS, Qdrant service, and PostgreSQL/pgvector HNSW.
benchmarks.target: Keep persisted FAISS and service-mode vector backends at recall@10 >= 0.95 while staying below 10 ms average query latency at 50000 vectors.
benchmarks.next_step: Use the dedicated production load profile for 100000 and 1000000-vector service tests, then tune pgvector and Qdrant for recall/latency.

benchmarks.name: Production pgvector tuning profile
benchmarks.dataset: Docker-backed PostgreSQL/pgvector tuning profile over 10000 and 50000 generated normalized 128-d vectors, with Qdrant service as the reference service baseline.
benchmarks.target: Use pgvector exact as the recall floor and keep pgvector iterative above recall@10 0.95 with p99 below 100 ms at 50000 vectors.
benchmarks.next_step: Promote pgvector iterative from the 50000-vector tuning profile into the 100k/1M production load SLO profiles when disk and build time allow it.

benchmarks.name: Production load profile 100k
benchmarks.dataset: 100000 generated normalized 128-d vectors; recall@10 measured against exact cosine neighbors.

WaveMind faiss-persisted.reason: Set WAVEMIND_FAISS_PATH to use the persisted FAISS backend

benchmarks.target: Reach recall@10 >= 0.95 and p99 latency < 100 ms on at least one production service backend at 100000 memories.
benchmarks.next_step: Keep at least one service backend at SLO pass while adding persisted FAISS from the Linux benchmark container and raising the target QPS.

benchmarks.name: Production load profile 1M
benchmarks.dataset: 1000000 generated normalized 128-d vectors; FAISS persisted and Qdrant service recall@10/latency profiles.
benchmarks.target: Keep recall@10 >= 0.95 and push p99 latency below 100 ms at 1M vectors.
benchmarks.next_step: Keep the FAISS persisted 1M profile green and tune Qdrant/pgvector so service-mode vector DB backends also pass recall and p99.

benchmarks.name: Qdrant 1M HNSW ef sweep
benchmarks.dataset: 1000000 generated normalized 128-d vectors; one Qdrant service collection queried with multiple hnsw_ef settings.
benchmarks.target: Find a setting that keeps recall@10 >= 0.95 while keeping p99 latency below 100 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Repeat with 100+ queries and collection-level HNSW build parameters; the current best recall setting still misses the p99 SLO.

benchmarks.name: Production streaming load runner
benchmarks.dataset: Memory-bounded streaming generator for 10M, 50M, and 100M target-recall load profiles. Checked-in artifacts include 10k smoke plus 100k, 1M, and 10M compressed FAISS IVF-PQ profiles, real Qdrant and PostgreSQL/pgvector service smokes, cold and tuned 1M Qdrant streaming profiles, strict single-service Qdrant, four-service sharded Qdrant, and four-service pgvector 10M results, a strict 50M FAISS IVF-PQ result, and a 100M sharded Qdrant preflight contract.

100k compressed / WaveMind faiss-ivfpq-p.memory_mode: streaming IVF-PQ; compressed codes plus query source vectors only

1M compressed / WaveMind faiss-ivfpq-per.memory_mode: streaming IVF-PQ; compressed codes plus query source vectors only

10M compressed / WaveMind faiss-ivfpq-pe.memory_mode: streaming IVF-PQ; compressed codes plus query source vectors only

50M preflight / WaveMind faiss-ivfpq-per.index_mode: persisted FAISS IVF-PQ compressed codes plus int64 ids

Qdrant smoke / Qdrant service streaming.memory_mode: streaming upsert; query source vectors only

1M Qdrant cold / Qdrant service streamin.memory_mode: streaming upsert; query source vectors only

1M Qdrant tuned / Qdrant service streami.memory_mode: streaming upsert; query source vectors only

10M Qdrant measured / Qdrant service str.memory_mode: streaming upsert; query source vectors only

Qdrant sharded smoke / Qdrant sharded se.memory_mode: horizontally sharded streaming upsert; parallel fanout query merge

10M Qdrant sharded measured / Qdrant sha.memory_mode: horizontally sharded streaming upsert; parallel fanout query merge

10M Qdrant preflight / Qdrant service st.index_mode: remote Qdrant service storage; local runner stores only generated batches

10M Qdrant sharded preflight / Qdrant sh.index_mode: remote horizontally sharded Qdrant storage; local runner routes ids across service URLs and fanout-merges top-k

100M Qdrant sharded preflight / Qdrant s.index_mode: remote horizontally sharded Qdrant storage; local runner routes ids across service URLs and fanout-merges top-k

pgvector smoke / WaveMind pgvector strea.memory_mode: streaming PostgreSQL insert; query source vectors only

10M pgvector preflight / WaveMind pgvect.index_mode: modulo-sharded PostgreSQL/pgvector services; local runner stores only generated batches

benchmarks.target: Keep strict 10M single-service and four-service sharded Qdrant, four-service pgvector, 10M/50M compressed FAISS, and service smokes green while completing 100M sharded Qdrant evidence.
benchmarks.next_step: Execute the 100M sharded Qdrant profile through production-streaming-load.yml on sized service infrastructure, then repeat pgvector on independent PostgreSQL nodes for HA evidence.

benchmarks.name: Scale readiness profile
benchmarks.dataset: Deterministic 1M-memory simulation for namespace placement, cluster autoscale planning, Kubernetes StatefulSet/CronJob/HPA generation, Knative/KEDA serverless plan generation, quorum runtime, service-mode replica repair, real HTTP shard transport, service-mode tombstone repair, anti-entropy repair worker, cursor-based active-active delta sync, sustained active-active mesh sync, field-only hotness delta sync, field-state CRDT convergence with actor-watermark health, replicated snapshot/offsite/archive restore, S3-compatible object-store upload verification, query-audit cache prewarm, query-vector cache, FastAPI batch query, Redis-compatible shared rate limiting, explicit recall feedback, batch recall feedback, Memory OS adaptive prewarm/consolidation/forgetting plus production architecture advice, hot-cache, API cache mutation safety, and structured-payload retrieval checks.
benchmarks.target: Prove the production foundation before heavier 100k, 1M, 10M, and 100M vector load tests: deterministic placement, cluster autoscale planning, full rolling rebalance batches with quorum/checkpoint/repair/validation, Kubernetes deployment, HPA autoscaling, serverless scale-to-zero planning, scheduled repair manifests, service-mode distributed namespace sharding, real HTTP shard transport, sustained mixed HTTP cluster load, missing-replica repair, tombstone-aware delete repair, anti-entropy repair worker, survivable replicas, cursor-based active-active sync, sustained active-active mesh convergence, field-only hotness sync, field-state convergence with actor-watermark missing/lag diagnostics, offsite/archive/object-store upload/latest-metadata/download/retention/DR-drill checks, query-vector cache, shared rate limiting, explicit and batch recall feedback, Memory OS adaptive prewarm/consolidation/forgetting with production architecture advice, hot-cache behavior, API cache mutation safety, structured payload recall, knowledge-graph traversal, and a 100M-memory capacity envelope.
benchmarks.next_step: Move from deterministic 100M capacity planning to service-backed 100M Qdrant/pgvector/FAISS load tests on sized hardware.

benchmarks.name: Postgres PITR runbook/preflight
benchmarks.dataset: Secret-safe database-native Postgres point-in-time recovery runbook: WAL archiving, streaming base backup, restore target config, replay verification, and promotion.
benchmarks.target: Keep Postgres source-of-truth recovery database-native: no embedded secrets, explicit WAL/archive/base-backup/restore/promotion steps, and a CLI preflight operators can run before production rollout.
benchmarks.next_step: Execute the same runbook against staging or managed Postgres and commit a real PITR drill report with replay LSN, target timestamp, restore duration, and post-restore row/index checks.

benchmarks.name: Production readiness gate
benchmarks.dataset: Gate generated from checked-in benchmark artifacts: production load SLO/cost, cluster placement, Kubernetes/operator output, serverless state externalization, cache/prewarm, query-vector cache, shared rate limiting, API cache mutation safety, Memory OS adaptive worker and embedded production architecture advice, distributed repair, cursor-based active-active CRDT convergence, backups, structured payloads, and 10M-load presence. External competitor-service evidence is tracked separately.
benchmarks.target: Reach readiness_score 1.0 with zero action_required items before claiming complete million-plus production readiness.
benchmarks.next_step: Keep the gate at readiness_score 1.0 while repeating larger service-backed runs and moving external competitor evidence into the separate adapter profile.

benchmarks.name: Production evidence environment contract
benchmarks.dataset: Secret-safe operator contract for strict production evidence. It maps required and recommended variables to claims, workflows, artifacts, GitHub Actions secrets, workflow inputs, and env templates without serializing credential values.
benchmarks.target: Keep every strict production evidence job mapped to explicit environment variables, GitHub secrets, workflow inputs, artifact outputs, and locked claims before any expensive remote/large-N run is launched.
benchmarks.next_step: Set the missing environment-scoped GitHub secrets, run the safe dispatch commands, and ingest downloaded artifacts through the strict evidence gate.

benchmarks.name: Strict evidence readiness runbook
benchmarks.dataset: Machine-readable join of strict production evidence, preflight, dispatch commands, scale plans, scale gaps, release claims, and leaderboard freshness. It proves the remaining 10M/50M/100M evidence gaps have exact safe launch, promotion, and validation steps without unlocking production claims.
benchmarks.target: Keep every strict evidence requirement mapped to a workflow, safe dispatch command, download command, ingest command, strict validation command, and locked claim boundary until real remote/large-N artifacts pass.
benchmarks.next_step: Provision the missing remote/service environments, run safe dispatch commands with commit_results=false, ingest downloaded artifacts, then rerun strict evidence validation before changing release claims.

benchmarks.name: Local HTTP cluster smoke
benchmarks.dataset: Four real localhost WaveMind API processes with isolated SQLite stores. The workload checks quorum writes, normal queries, simulated node failover queries, missing-replica repair, replicated forget, delete suppression, p99, and SLO status.
benchmarks.target: Keep success_rate, failover_hit_rate, delete_suppression_rate, and cluster health at 1.0; use a 2000 ms p99 ceiling for GitHub runner variance while keeping checked-in local evidence below 1000 ms before promoting remote service-node deployments.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same workload against external service nodes and then increase namespace count and payload size on sized hardware.

benchmarks.name: Local HTTP active-active service-region smoke
benchmarks.dataset: Three real localhost WaveMind API region processes. Each region runs a replicated WaveMind runtime behind FastAPI; the workload checks namespace-delta export/import, cursor idempotency, convergence, delete propagation, final no-op sync, p99 operation latency, and SLO status.
benchmarks.target: Keep real API-process convergence, delete suppression, and pair-sync success at 1.0 with final no-op imports at 0 before moving the same workload to remote Kubernetes or serverless regions.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same active-active service-region workload against remote Kubernetes/serverless API nodes with external Postgres/Qdrant/Redis state.

benchmarks.name: External HTTP cluster load runner
benchmarks.dataset: Real WaveMind HTTP API-node sustained workload: quorum writes, normal queries, simulated node failover queries, missing-replica repair, replicated forget, delete suppression, external /query/batch recall, and SLO verdict over user-supplied node URLs.
benchmarks.target: Keep the service-node workload and external batch recall green against real API processes, then repeat it against remote Kubernetes or serverless nodes before claiming external-cluster production readiness.
benchmarks.next_step: Replace the current loopback service-node artifact with a remote node manifest run from a multi-node deployment.

benchmarks.name: Kubernetes operator leader failover smoke
benchmarks.dataset: A four-node kind cluster with two WaveMind operator replicas. The runner deletes the current Lease holder, requires Kubernetes Lease takeover, verifies resourceVersion transition accounting, scales the WaveMind StatefulSet through the new leader, deletes a data pod, and checks API recovery.

WaveMind Kubernetes operator failover.claim_boundary: Ephemeral multi-node Kubernetes CI evidence. It proves real Kubernetes API reconciliation, Lease/etcd-backed operator failover, StatefulSet reconciliation, and pod recovery. It does not unlock remote production, multi-region, managed-serverless, or 100M admission claims.

benchmarks.target: Pass real Kubernetes Lease-holder deletion, follower takeover, post-failover reconcile, StatefulSet scaling, data-pod replacement, and API recovery on every relevant main push.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same failure drill against a non-ephemeral remote Kubernetes staging cluster and feed the resulting endpoints into cluster admission.

benchmarks.name: Kubernetes service-network physical worker failure smoke
benchmarks.dataset: A deterministic 256-memory workload distributed across 32 namespaces and four real WaveMind pod-DNS API endpoints in three kind worker zones. The runner physically pauses one worker container, requires the network probe to detect the unavailable replica, verifies 100% quorum recall during the outage, unpauses the worker, and requires full service recovery.

WaveMind Kubernetes physical worker fail.claim_boundary: Ephemeral non-loopback Kubernetes service-network and physical kind-worker failure evidence; not remote multi-region production admission.

benchmarks.target: Keep 100% deterministic quorum recall through a physical worker outage across non-loopback pod DNS, then recover every service node without replacing persistent pods.
benchmarks.next_step: Repeat the same protocol on a non-ephemeral remote Kubernetes staging cluster, then run node, zone, and region drills through strict cluster admission.

benchmarks.name: Kubernetes active-active region failure and recovery smoke
benchmarks.dataset: Three PVC-backed replicated WaveMind regions placed in three Kubernetes worker zones. The runner converges 48 initial writes, physically pauses the region-b worker, performs 32 writes plus a delete while region-b is unavailable, verifies survivor convergence, restores the worker, and requires full convergence, tombstone suppression, and an idempotent final sync.

WaveMind Kubernetes active-active region.claim_boundary: Ephemeral non-loopback Kubernetes three-zone active-active region failure evidence; not remote multi-region production admission.

benchmarks.target: Keep write availability, convergence, and delete suppression at 1.00 through physical region loss and recovery without replacing the region PVC pod.
benchmarks.next_step: Run this protocol across independent remote Kubernetes regions and use that external artifact for strict active-active admission.

benchmarks.name: Kubernetes serverless external-state lifecycle smoke
benchmarks.dataset: A four-node, three-zone kind deployment with PVC-backed PostgreSQL, Qdrant, and Redis plus stateless WaveMind workers. It scales API replicas 1-to-0-to-1-to-3-to-0-to-1, verifies durable recall, bounded cross-replica write/delete visibility, and a concurrent burst SLO.

WaveMind Kubernetes serverless lifecycle.claim_boundary: Ephemeral non-loopback Kubernetes lifecycle evidence with external durable state. It proves scale-to-zero state safety and multi-replica behavior, but does not unlock remote managed Knative/KEDA production admission.

benchmarks.target: Keep both scale-to-zero restores at 1.00, write/delete visibility at 3/3 within 2000 ms, and burst p99 below 2000 ms on every relevant main push.
benchmarks.next_step: Repeat the same lifecycle on managed Knative/KEDA endpoints and ingest remote telemetry through strict serverless admission.

benchmarks.name: Kubernetes PostgreSQL backup and Qdrant rebuild DR smoke
benchmarks.dataset: A checksummed PostgreSQL custom-format backup from the serverless lifecycle namespace. Source state services are stopped, the archive is restored into fresh PVC-backed PostgreSQL in an independent namespace, and an empty Qdrant collection is rebuilt before recall and API-pod replacement checks.

WaveMind Kubernetes PostgreSQL/Qdrant DR.claim_boundary: Ephemeral non-loopback Kubernetes disaster-recovery evidence. It proves logical PostgreSQL backup/restore and Qdrant rebuild in an independent namespace, not managed-cloud PITR or multi-region DR.

benchmarks.target: Restore 100% of memories into an independent namespace, rebuild Qdrant exactly from PostgreSQL, survive recovery API replacement, and finish within 180 seconds.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same protocol against managed PostgreSQL backups, object storage, and a remote Kubernetes recovery cluster before claiming managed-cloud PITR or multi-region DR.

benchmarks.name: External HTTP active-active loopback
benchmarks.dataset: Real localhost WaveMind API regions passed into the external URL-based active-active runner. This checks the same namespace-delta export/import, cursor idempotency, convergence, delete propagation, final no-op sync, p99, and SLO contract used for user-supplied remote region URLs.
benchmarks.target: Keep the URL-based active-active contract green in CI while remote Kubernetes/serverless regions are provisioned.
benchmarks.next_step: Use the same runner path against real remote API regions through `.github/workflows/external-http-active-active.yml`.

benchmarks.name: External HTTP active-active region runner
benchmarks.dataset: User-supplied remote WaveMind API regions. The workflow runs the same active-active namespace-delta workload as the local service smoke and writes benchmarks/external_http_active_active_results.json when real regions are configured.

WaveMind real HTTP active-active service.reason: Run external-http-active-active with real region URLs or a regions manifest.

benchmarks.target: Check in a real remote Kubernetes/serverless active-active region result with convergence, delete suppression, and success at 1.0, final no-op imports at 0, and p99 below 1500 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Run workflow_dispatch external-http-active-active against at least three remote API regions backed by external Postgres/Qdrant/Redis state.

benchmarks.name: External multimodal evidence runner
benchmarks.dataset: User-supplied external multimodal manifest with shared-space asset/query vectors, s3 object-store URIs, verified sha256/byte-size metadata, encode p95 values, and target relevance labels.
benchmarks.target: Generate benchmarks/multimodal_external_encoder_results.json from a real external CLIP/audio/video/3D encoder manifest with >=1000 payloads, >=200 queries, precision@1 >=0.90, cross-modal precision@1 >=0.90, object-store verification >=0.99, and p99 <=250 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the external multimodal manifest path against real s3-backed assets and then let wavemind multimodal-admission evaluate the produced artifact.

benchmarks.name: Memory OS policy evolution
benchmarks.dataset: Deterministic multi-cycle Memory OS workload with repeated query-audit traffic, production-scale target settings, policy-history audit events, and scheduler replanning.
benchmarks.target: Keep multi-cycle policy history passing with full decision coverage, repeated required-policy escalation, stable OK policy detection, scheduler escalation, hot-query prewarm, predictive prefetch, and priority learning.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the same policy-evolution benchmark against a real Redis-backed staging namespace and promote the artifact only after memory-os-admission remains plan-limited by external production evidence rather than local worker behavior.

benchmarks.name: Memory OS runtime policy bundle
benchmarks.dataset: Checked-in Memory OS canary, policy-evolution, and admission artifacts converted into a deterministic operator-applied runtime policy manifest with staging promotion and production lock checks.
benchmarks.target: Keep the bundle staging-promotable when canary/evolution pass, keep production locked while memory-os-admission is plan-only, and expose Kubernetes/env policy patches without enabling unattended production automation.
benchmarks.next_step: Apply the bundle to a Redis-backed staging namespace, replay real tenant traffic, then rerun canary, policy bundle, and memory-os-admission from that environment.

benchmarks.name: Memory competitor adapter profile
benchmarks.dataset: Generated dynamic-memory adapter profile covering 50 users, 450 facts, 300 checks, correction, TTL, namespace isolation, preferences, and token validity.

Zep.reason: Set ZEP_API_URL or ZEP_API_KEY to run the Zep adapter profile.

GraphRAG static graph.backend: local lexical entity graph over active facts

benchmarks.target: Keep Mem0, LangGraph, and GraphRAG-style generated dynamic adapter results checked in, then check in a live Zep Cloud or OSS-compatible service run once ZEP_API_URL or ZEP_API_KEY is configured.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the live Zep adapter against a configured service and raise the generated profile to 1000+ facts once optional competitor dependencies are stable in CI.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval answer generation
benchmarks.dataset: LongMemEval-S questions answered from compact retrieved evidence. Checked-in local runs compare WaveMind, Chroma, and Qdrant using Ollama qwen2.5:0.5b and qwen2.5:1.5b over the first 50 non-abstention questions.
benchmarks.target: Move from lightweight local smoke runs to full LongMemEval-S answer generation with stronger local and API models, then compare against Chroma/Qdrant RAG.
benchmarks.next_step: Run all 470 non-abstention questions with a stronger local/API model and add LLM-judge faithfulness scoring.

benchmarks.name: BEIR
benchmarks.dataset: Heterogeneous zero-shot information retrieval benchmark across many public datasets.
benchmarks.target: On identical embeddings, stay within 0.02 nDCG@10 of Chroma/Qdrant and keep WaveMind reranking latency below 10 ms.
benchmarks.next_step: Implement a BEIR adapter for SciFact and NFCorpus first because they are small enough for local CI smoke runs.

benchmarks.name: MTEB Retrieval
benchmarks.dataset: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark retrieval tasks across many datasets and languages.
benchmarks.target: Use MTEB to separate encoder quality from memory-policy quality; WaveMind should not reduce same-embedding retrieval quality.
benchmarks.next_step: Run a small retrieval subset with hash, sentence-transformers, and a modern multilingual embedding model.

benchmarks.name: MIRACL Russian
benchmarks.dataset: Native-speaker judged multilingual retrieval benchmark with Russian included. NoMIRACL Russian compact candidate benchmark is implemented; full-corpus MIRACL remains planned.
benchmarks.target: Prove Russian recall with semantic embeddings; target nDCG@10 parity with same-embedding Chroma/Qdrant.
benchmarks.next_step: Extend the NoMIRACL loader to full MIRACL Russian corpus once disk/service capacity is available.

benchmarks.name: VectorDBBench
benchmarks.dataset: VectorDBBench custom dataset export with train/test/neighbors/scalar-label parquet files. Checked-in manifest points to a reproducible 10000-vector, 100-query, 128-d cosine dataset.
benchmarks.target: Use official VectorDBBench runs to compare WaveMind's production index paths without pretending NumPy local mode is a cloud vector database.
benchmarks.next_step: Run the generated custom dataset through official VectorDBBench targets for Qdrant, Milvus, pgvector, and WaveMind-backed FAISS/Qdrant profiles.

benchmarks.name: LoCoMo answer generation
benchmarks.dataset: LoCoMo questions answered from retrieved evidence with an LLM.
benchmarks.target: Beat static vector-store RAG on temporal/correction questions by at least 15 percentage points while returning compact evidence.
benchmarks.next_step: After the retrieval-only LoCoMo run is published, add an optional Ollama answer-generation layer using the user's installed local model.

benchmarks.name: LongMemEval-V2
benchmarks.dataset: Web-agent histories with state recall, dynamic state tracking, workflow knowledge, gotchas, and premise awareness.
benchmarks.target: Become a compact evidence retriever for agent trajectories, with explicit wins on dynamic state tracking and gotcha recall.
benchmarks.next_step: Prototype trajectory-to-memory segmentation and compare compact WaveMind evidence against plain chunk retrieval.

benchmarks.name: LMEB
benchmarks.dataset: Long-horizon Memory Embedding Benchmark across episodic, dialogue, semantic, and procedural memory tasks.
benchmarks.target: Use LMEB to choose the default semantic encoder and prove that memory retrieval is not just passage retrieval.
benchmarks.next_step: Run LMEB retrieval tasks once optional dataset downloads are available outside restricted CI.

benchmarks.name: RAGBench
benchmarks.dataset: Large RAG evaluation dataset with industry-style corpora and explainable labels.
benchmarks.target: Show whether WaveMind's dynamic suppression improves context quality when facts become stale or conflicting.
benchmarks.next_step: Add an optional RAGBench adapter after retrieval benchmarks are stable.
